title: Build a Recipe Club with Protected Recipes

content.problemStatement:
In this practice problem, you will build a website for a Recipe Club, where anyone can browse the homepage and read basic cooking tips, but only registered and logged-in users can view exclusive premium recipes.

The homepage (/) should display:
- A welcome message and some basic cooking tips (public content)
- Three links:
   - Register for Premium Account [(`/register/`) should allow new users to sign u]
   - Login to Premium [(`/login/`) should allow users to log in.]
   - View Premium Recipes [(`/recipes/`) should display exclusive recipe content and should be accessible only to logged-in users.]
- If an unauthenticated user tries to access /recipes/, they should be redirected to an Access Denied page (/access-denied/), which tells them they need to log in.
Everthing has been set up for you except the middleware script.

### Task:
1. Complete the script in `middleware.py`
2. Include the class `RecipeAccessMiddleware` to settings.py under MIDDLEWARE.

After completing the above, click on **RUN** to start the server.
- Open the browser in a new tab.
- Try navigating to premium recipes without logging in - you should see a access denied page
- Create a user by using the REGISTER link and then LOGIN
- See if you can navigate to premium recipes now - you should be able to.
